title: tdd run-state checkpoint
description: Eagerly-written, branch-committed checkpoint that lets a run resume after a limit-pause (Claude or Codex) without losing progress. Lives at .parallax/<slug>/run-state.json. See commands/run.md -> 'Limits, checkpointing & resume'.

run_id.description: Unique id for this run (stable across resumes).

slug.description: Feature slug; the run lives on feature/<slug>.

epic.description: The integration/epic branch this feature targets (e.g. feature/flex-cis).

base_tip.description: origin/<epic> commit sha the run started from (provenance anchor).

feature_id.description: v0.31: stable feature UUID (matches feature-state.json). Absent in v0.30 artifacts (migration assigns one).

contract_generation.description: v0.31: contract generation this run belongs to. Absent => generation 1 (v0.30 backward-compat).

parent_run_id.description: v0.31: run_id of the previous generation, or null for generation 1.

resolution_queue.description: v0.31: path to .parallax/<slug>/resolution-queue.json (the structured spec-gap source of truth).

resolution_chain.description: v0.31: applied resolution batch ids leading to this generation.

verified_tree.description: Feature-level receipt bound to the committed feature tip: the content hash of ALL tracked non-.parallax/ files (code+tests+config) at the tip when the run COMPLETED and every slice was verified (canonical form: `git ls-tree -r <tip> | grep -v <TAB>.parallax/ | git hash-object --stdin`). scripts/epic-gate.py recomputes this from the promoted feature commit and requires equality, so a code change after review (which leaves the per-slice ledgers untouched) is mechanically caught. REQUIRED once status=complete.

status.description: Run-level status. A resume that finds 'complete' no-ops and self-terminates the schedule. v0.31 adds needs-resolution (parked on a confirmed spec-gap), resolving (a /parallax:resolve transaction is in flight), and abandoned.

paused.description: Set when status is paused-on-limit; null otherwise.

service.description: Which service's limit caused the pause.

retry_after.description: ISO timestamp the limit error said to retry after, if any; a smart scheduler prefers this over blind hourly.

slices.description: Per-slice progress. A resume skips 'integrated', runs only the owed verification for 'green-unverified', and continues 'in_progress'/'pending'.

status.description: green-unverified = arbiter passed but the cross-model verification is still owed (e.g. paused on a Codex limit); NOT integrated.

iterations.description: Iteration counter, so the circuit breaker (max 3) survives a resume.

parked_reason.description: Set when status is parked: spec-gap | breaker | divergence.

code_tip.description: SHA of the slice's code track branch tip — so a resume continues 'in_progress' from the exact committed point, not a guess.

test_tip.description: SHA of the slice's test track branch tip.

wave_base.description: SHA of the integration tip this slice's tracks forked from — the diff base for parallel integration (git diff wave_base..tip | git apply --3way). Required once a slice is in_progress or green-unverified.

wave.description: Dependency-DAG wave this slice belongs to.

review_ledger.description: Path to this slice's review ledger (.parallax/<slug>/reviews/<id>.json). The ledger's rounds_used is the SINGLE source of truth for the review budget — run-state deliberately keeps no second counter that could diverge from it.

arbiter_verdict.description: The arbiter's owed GREEN for a 'green-unverified' slice — so a resume runs ONLY the pending cross-model verification, not the whole slice.

verified_diff.description: SHA/hash of the assembled diff the verdict was made against — a resume re-verifies the same tree it paused on, and can detect drift.

integrated.description: Ids of slices merged into feature/<slug>.

escalations.description: Path to .parallax/<slug>/escalations.md (genuine ambiguity only — never limit-pauses).

reviews_dir.description: Path to .parallax/<slug>/reviews/ — one ledger file per slice (<id>.json; schema assets/codex/review-ledger.schema.json), so a slice cannot spend another's review budget.

updated_at.description: ISO timestamp of this checkpoint write.

lock.description: Mutual-exclusion lease so two concurrent resumes (e.g. overlapping hourly fires) cannot run the same slug at once. The ATOMIC primitive is a branch ref refs/heads/<branch_prefix>lock/<slug> pointing at a UNIQUE lock commit (run_id baked in via git commit-tree) — taken create-if-absent locally and by 'git push --force-with-lease=<ref>:' in the cloud (so two fresh same-HEAD clones cannot both win); this object records it for visibility. A resume refuses to start while a live (unexpired) lock is held; it may steal an expired one.

acquired_at.description: ISO timestamp the lease was taken.

expires_at.description: ISO timestamp; after this a resume may steal the lease (the holder is presumed dead).

completion.description: v0.37.1 terminal completion receipt. REQUIRED once status==complete: it binds this run-state to the exact terminal evidence bundle being finalized — not merely a non-empty updated_at. scripts/finalize-gate.py recomputes the evidence sha256s and the code-tree hash from the committed feature ref and requires every field here to match.

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp; finalize-gate.py parses it as ISO (a non-ISO value like 't' is rejected).

verified_tree.description: Must equal top-level verified_tree and the recomputed code-tree hash of the committed ref.

terminal_event.description: Exactly run_completed — the terminal event that must also be present in events.jsonl for this run.
